stories.title: Trump made more than $1bn from crypto in first year back in office
stories.summary: The president earned millions more from real estate, Trump-themed Bibles, watches and other items.

stories.title: Anthropic says US lifts export ban on its advanced AI tools
stories.summary: Fable and Mythos were abruptly suspended in June over concerns that they could be used by hackers.

stories.title: Anthropic says Trump admin has lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5
stories.summary: The U.S. Department of Commerce lifted the export controls on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models

stories.title: White House lifts ban on Anthropic models
stories.summary: US government move allows AI start-up to re-release Mythos and Fable models

stories.title: Gold Steadies as US-Iran Peace Talks Frame Inflation Outlook
stories.summary: Gold was steady, with traders assessing peace talks between the US and Iran and weighing a fresh slew of US economic data for clues on the Federal Reserve’s next move on interest rates.

stories.title: US Lifts Export Restrictions on Anthropic’s Fable 5 AI Model
stories.summary: The US government removed foreign access restrictions on Anthropic PBC’s Fable 5 artificial intelligence model, clearing it for wider distribution after the startup resolved the Trump administration’s safety concerns.

stories.title: What’s driving China’s retailers to bet on own brands? Profit growth, price control: S&P
stories.summary: China’s biggest retailers are increasingly betting on private-label products as a long-term engine for profit growth and customer loyalty, with the category forecast to double its share of the country’s fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) market over the next eight years, according to S&P Global Ratings. “Private labels are becoming a more important profit driver for Chinese retailers as supplier-fu

stories.title: Record chip rally adds $2 trillion in combined value to Micron, Intel and AMD in second quarter
stories.summary: Wall Street poured into chipmakers not named Nvidia in the second quarter, as the artificial intelligence boom expanded to include more suppliers.

stories.title: Oil Edges Up as Peace Talks Continue, Hormuz Traffic Recovers
stories.summary: Oil inched higher after the biggest quarterly drop since the pandemic, as traders monitored peace talks between the US and Iran and the return of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Anthropic launches AI drug discovery program, joining tech giants in betting on healthcare
stories.summary: Anthropic will start an internal drug discovery program, part of a new push to sell artificial intelligence tools to drugmakers.

stories.title: Former Treasury Secretary Lew on Iran Talks, US-Israel Relations
stories.summary: Jack Lew, former US Treasury Secretary and former US ambassador to Israel, says economic sanctions on Iran can be effective but need to be used with care. Speaking on "Bloomberg The Close," Lew also comments on the US dollar and the Israel-Lebanon pact. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: US Says Witkoff, Kushner Had Positive Talks in Doha on Iran Deal
stories.summary: US negotiators Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff had positive discussions with regional leaders in Qatar and technical talks with Iran are moving ahead, according to a senior administration official, as the countries seek to ease tensions in the wake of recent attacks that imperiled an already fragile ceasefire.

stories.title: Trump's annual financial disclosure shows more than $580M in crypto-related income
stories.summary: Trump's 927-page financial disclosure report for 2025 totals more than 900 pages detailing holdings in cryptocurrency and stocks.

stories.title: Asian Stocks Rise After US Tech Gains, Yen Steady: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Asian equities rose Wednesday after capping their best quarter in 17 years, as a rally in chipmakers and signs of US economic resilience fueled optimism about corporate earnings.

stories.title: Orban Steered More Contracts to Allies Before Loss, Report Says
stories.summary: The government of former Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban was directing an ever greater share of spending toward politically-connected companies in the two years leading up to a landslide election defeat, a report from an anti-graft body showed.

stories.title: Bloomberg Crypto 6/30/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg Crypto" covers the people, transactions, and technology shaping the world of decentralized finance. Today's guests: Kalshi Head of Enforcement Robert DeNault, BitGet CEO Gracy Chen, Luxor Technology COO Ethan Vera, and Fireblocks Policy Director Dea Markova. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Alcoa Bets on Aluminum Boom With $5.6 Billion South32 Deal
stories.summary: Alcoa Corp. has agreed to buy South32 Ltd.’s bauxite, alumina and aluminum assets in a deal worth as much as $5.6 billion, cementing its position as a top producer as long-term demand strengthens and the Iran war exposes supply concerns.

stories.title: Progress Software Earnings Beat Estimates, Boosts Forecast
stories.summary: Yogesh Gupta, CEO of Progress Software, said that AI is a big driver for his company as it beat earnings estimates and boosts forecasts for the next quarter. Gupta said that his company can aggregate data for AI in a way that reduces the number of tokens necessary to run AI agents and saves customers money. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Trump Reports at Least $1.4 Billion in 2025 Crypto Earnings
stories.summary: President Donald Trump reported earning at least $1.4 billion in 2025 from crypto and memecoin-related businesses, according to his latest annual financial disclosure.

stories.title: China’s Factory Activity Returns to Growth | The China Show 6/30/2026
stories.summary: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, Yvonne Man and Avril Hong give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Don't See Slowdown in Institutional Client Interest: Markova
stories.summary: Dea Markova, policy director at Fireblocks, joins Isabelle Lee on "Bloomberg Crypto." They discuss the expected updated draft to the Clarity Act that may be released soon. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: New Crypto Bill Draft Expected Soon
stories.summary: Bloomberg Intelligences Nathan Dean joins Isabelle Lee on "Bloomberg Crypto." One of the Republican senators spearheading efforts to pass an industry-backed measure to regulate cryptocurrency and digital assets said Thursday lawmakers plan to soon release an “updated draft” of the bill that seeks to address members’ concerns over ethics. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: CIA Aims to Speed Up Tech Adoption as AI Is ‘Rewriting’ Conflict
stories.summary: CIA Director John Ratcliffe vowed to step up the agency’s efforts to deploy artificial intelligence and quantum computing, stressing that rapid developments in emerging technologies are changing the nature of geopolitics.

stories.title: Bitcoin Miners Valuable Asset for Electric Grid: Vera
stories.summary: Ethan Vera, COO of Luxor Technology, joins Isabelle Lee on "Bloomberg Crypto." Many miners are shifting their focus to AI. More broadly, crypto firms are beginning to apply the infrastructure and market expertise they built around digital assets to AI, from converting mining sites into data centers to building markets for GPUs and computing power. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Anthropic launches Claude Science in push for pharma revenue
stories.summary: New AI product has use cases in rendering 3D protein structures and drug discovery

stories.title: Cleveland Fed President Hammack says AI could fuel inflation, rate hikes may be necessary
stories.summary: "We've got inflation that's too high, and it's been too high for the past five years," Beth Hammack told CNBC's Sara Eisen.

stories.title: UK investors sue Binance and founder Zhao Changpeng for US$200 million
stories.summary: Almost 1,700 British ⁠investors are suing ⁠Binance and founder ⁠Zhao Changpeng for at least £150 million (US$200 million), alleging the crypto trading platform sold them risky, complex derivative products without regulatory authorisation. The claimants, some ‌of whom said they had lost tens of thousands of pounds, allege Binance entities knowingly sold investments such as leveraged products, which

stories.title: Aspiring Nvidia Rival Gets Backing From Jane Street
stories.summary: Startup Etched is emerging from stealth with an eye towards taking on chip heavyweight Nvidia, as the industry shifts from training AI models to running them. The company has raised $800 million in funding including backing from Jane Street and TSMC-linked firm Venture Tech Alliance. Etched CEO Gavin Uberti sits down with Ed Ludlow for more on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Chip Stocks on Track for Best Quarter Ever | Bloomberg Tech 6/30/2026
stories.summary: Bloomberg’s Ed Ludlow breaks down what's behind the chip stock rally, with the sector heading toward its best quarter ever. Plus, Taiwan raids the offices of Super Micro as part of an investigation into the alleged smuggling of Nvidia chips into China. And, how a billion-dollar misunderstanding has left Korean investors empty-handed in the blockbuster SpaceX IPO. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Science for Automating Research
stories.summary: Anthropic PBC is releasing new software aimed at helping scientists automate research, in the hopes of reducing some of the tedious aspects of their work.

stories.title: Trump administration's AI crackdown opens door for China to close gap
stories.summary: The Trump administration's crackdown on Anthropic's leading artificial intelligence models is looking like a gift to China.

stories.title: Kotak Reclaims India ECM Lead With Mega Deals on Horizon

stories.title: Trump Reports Earning $26 Million From Foreign Real Estate Deals
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said he had earned at least $26 million in income from an array of new real estate ventures abroad, illustrating how his fortune has grown as his family embraces foreign deal-making in his second term.

stories.title: Plea for households to read energy meter as prices rise
stories.summary: Household energy prices have risen by 13% a year as regulator Ofgem's latest price cap kicks in.

stories.title: Rep. LaLota Sees Path to Limiting Birthright Citizenship
stories.summary: Representative Nick LaLota, a New York Republican and member of the House Appropriations and Homeland Security committees, says he was “a little disappointed” by the Supreme Court’s birthright citizenship ruling but believes the opinion leaves room for Congress to act. LaLota says he sees a path for lawmakers to pursue legislation “to narrow the scope” and limit birthright citizenship to those law

stories.summary: CNBC's Jim Cramer said Wall Street is rewarding the companies supplying the artificial intelligence boom rather than the technology giants funding it.

stories.title: Nike results top estimates even as China sales drop 12%; retailer expects $986 million tariff refund
stories.summary: Nike was expected to report another quarter of declining sales as the sneaker giant struggles to regain strength under its turnaround strategy.

stories.title: Michael Burry says he's shorting Caterpillar for the first time after it nearly doubled in the AI-driven rally of 2026
stories.summary: "Caterpillar jumped out at me," Burry said. "I have never shorted Caterpillar. It has always done great for me on the long side in the past."

stories.summary: Parent company EchoStar has surged to $30bn market capitalisation from windfall on SpaceX holding

stories.title: Oil Market Still Regulating Despite 'Flare Ups' Says Samantha Dart
stories.summary: Samantha Dart, co-head of global commodities research at Goldman Sachs, said that markets haven't reacted to 'flare ups' in the Strait of Hormuz because US energy exports and China imports have remained consistent, signaling the oil market is still headed in the right direction. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Debt and Risky Bets Fuel South Korea and Taiwan's AI Stock Boom
stories.summary: On today’s Big Take Asia podcast, how an AI-driven stock boom in Taiwan and South Korea is enticing investors to take big risks on one of the world’s hottest market rallies.

stories.title: iHerb Is Said to Select Banks for $500 Million IPO This Year
stories.summary: iHerb, an online retailer focused on vitamins and supplements, has picked banks to lead its initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.title: Kura Sushi to Accelerate US Growth to Fend Off Rivals’ Expansion
stories.summary: Conveyor-belt sushi operator Kura Sushi Inc. has an ambitious target to grow its store count by 20% a year in the US, betting that it can build market share before a wave of competitors rolls in.

stories.title: Nike Sees Weakness Persisting as Wall Street Patience Wears Thin

stories.title: Witkoff, Kushner to Hold Indirect Talks With Iran
stories.summary: Qatar said Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff had arrived in Doha as part of ongoing peace negotiations between the US and Iran. Qatari officials say they won't meet directly with the Iranians. Jeff Mason reports from the White House. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Japanese yen sinks to 40-year low, keeping intervention risks in focus
stories.summary: The Japanese yen weakened to its lowest level against the U.S. dollar since 1986 on Tuesday, keeping investors on alert for possible intervention from Japanese authorities.

stories.title: US Sanctions Mexican Companies for Cartel-Linked Fuel Smuggling
stories.summary: The US Treasury announced sanctions against two Mexican citizens and nine companies in the country that the agency said are linked to a fuel-smuggling scheme with ties to a cartel.

Then, Bloomberg's Ian King dives into semiconductor rally as chips send stocks to their best quarter in six years. Plus, Dan Suzuki, global investment strategist at iCapital, said that market tailwinds are in the rearview and that he

stories.title: Canadian Stocks Beating US for a Second Year as Banks Surge
stories.summary: Canada’s equity benchmark is positioned to outperform its US counterpart for a second year in a row — which would be its first back-to-back win in 15 years.

stories.title: Bitget CEO Discusses Tokenization, Market-Making
stories.summary: Gracy Chen, CEO of Bitget, joins Isabelle Lee on "Bloomberg Crypto." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Securitize Set to Go Public Thursday
stories.summary: Bloomberg Intelligence's Dushyant Shahrawat joins Isabelle Lee on "Bloomberg Crypto." Securitize debuts on the New York Stock Exchange this Thursday after investors approved its SPAC merger, which will make it one of the first publicly traded pure-play tokenization companies. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: High Interest in Perpetual Futures: Kalshi's DeNault
stories.summary: Robert DeNault, head of enforcement at Kalshi, joins Isabelle Lee on "Bloomberg Crypto." Last month the CFTC permitted the listing of perpetual futures on US exchanges. Kalshi’s contracts were the first perpetual futures approved by the CFTC, marking an expansion beyond the company’s core business of listing event contracts. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Bitcoin ETFs on Track for Record Monthly Outflow
stories.summary: Bloomberg Intelligence's James Seyffart joins Isabelle Lee on "Bloomberg Crypto." Bitcoin’s biggest institutional sources of demand are faltering at the same time, leaving the cryptocurrency increasingly exposed as ETF investors pull money at a record pace and questions persist over the financing playbook behind its largest corporate buyer. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Saylor's Strategy May Sell Up to $1.25 Billion of Bitcoin
stories.summary: Bloomberg's Monique Mulima joins Isabelle Lee on "Bloomberg Crypto." Michael Saylor’s Strategy unveiled a sweeping overhaul of the financing model underpinning its Bitcoin strategy, giving itself broader powers to sell the cryptocurrency, buy back securities and preserve liquidity as it adapts to mounting pressure on the structure that fueled years of aggressive accumulation. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Carney Concedes Canada Will Overshoot Emissions Forecasts
stories.summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney said Canada’s emissions will be higher in the coming years than previously projected, his clearest admission yet that the country’s near-term climate targets are likely out of reach.

stories.title: Binance and founder sued by crypto investors in London lawsuit

stories.title: Bitcoin Slides Below $60,000 as Strategy Selloff Refuels Anxiety
stories.summary: Bitcoin tumbled as much as 3.5% toward $58,000 on Tuesday as investors reversed an initial vote of confidence in Michael Saylor’s financing overhaul at Strategy Inc., raising fresh concerns that one of the cryptocurrency’s biggest buyers may no longer be a consistent source of demand.

stories.title: ASML, Chip-Tool Stocks Soar as Samsung, SK Plan Investments
stories.summary: Shares of companies that supply semiconductor equipment are soaring this week as increased investments from leading chipmakers in South Korea bolster investors’ optimism.

stories.summary: Investors are growing jittery about massive AI spending by the Mag 7 and when these companies will produce a return on that investment.

stories.summary: The European Union set an October deadline for “tangible results” in tackling its growing trade deficit with China, after the two sides held talks in Brussels in an attempt to avoid a trade war. “Not everything will be solved, not everything will be fixed, but we think that between now and October, our teams have sufficient time to deliver the tangible results,” EU trade chief Maros Sefcovic told

stories.title: Inflation likely to stay ‘significantly above target,’ top European central banker warns
stories.summary: Joachim Nagel, president of Germany’s Bundesbank, spoke to CNBC on the sidelines of an ECB conference in Portugal.

stories.summary: Cal-Maine Foods, Versova and Hickman's Egg Ranch agreed to settle allegations of price manipulation by the Department of Justice and 17 states.

stories.summary: The way European banks fund themselves is quietly shifting. Geopolitical fragmentation, rising financing costs and increasingly unstable global energy supplies are pushing lenders to look beyond their traditional capital markets. stories.title: Short sellers keep betting against Pop Mart — even though it's been a losing trade
stories.summary: Short sellers are doubling down on Pop Mart International even as a recent share price recovery turns their bearish bets on the Chinese toymaker increasingly risky.
